verdict_reasoning: EREG (Epiregulin) is a member of the EGF family encoded as a type-I single-pass transmembrane precursor (pro-epiregulin) that is expressed on the cell surface before ectodomain shedding by ADAM10/ADAM17 releases the soluble mature EGF-like ligand. The TM-anchored precursor stage supports juxtacrine signaling at the plasma membrane, but the dominant biological actor is the shed soluble form that activates EGFR and ErbB4. Because the membrane-anchored TM precursor stage is real but transient and the shed soluble ligand is the canonical signaling form, this maps to `contextual` / `dual_localization` (transient TM proligand whose ectodomain is rapidly shed). EREG is also overexpressed in colorectal and other cancers, where the surface precursor pool may be more prominent. The protein does not have a stable, persistent surface residence comparable to a canonical surface receptor.
